view_meta: AI sessions as live, replayable typed runs — every step, tool call, gate and verdict
purpose: Replaces the thin Sessions list with a two-pane WORKFLOWS run view: a compact band-striped run list on the left, and a Temporal-style vertical typed timeline of the selected AI run on the right — ingested → correlate → rag-context → classify(band) → prediction.commit → agent-loop (nested tool calls) → screen → prediction-gate → execute → verify(verdict) — with live advancing, breathing active node, sticky instrument header, and every actuation control gated behind MUTATION OFF.
notes:
Design decisions, faithful to the TG color language:
- Epistemic hue discipline: every payload block carries a left border encoding its source — indigo (--map) for AI claims (classify reasoning, committed plans, screen/gate rationale), steel (--territory) for observed telemetry (ingest, correlate, rag facts, tool-call results). The verify node renders an explicit MAP vs TERRITORY comparison grid, and the verdict (match/partial/deviation) colors both its chip and its timeline dot.
- Status is never color-alone: dots vary in SHAPE (filled circle=done, dashed circle=pending, thick ring=paused-for-human, rotated diamond=FLOORED hazard, square=deviation/failed) and every chip pairs a glyph with an uppercase label.
- Bands: left stripe on each run row + chip in the sticky header use --auto/--notice/--pause; the never-auto floor renders in --hazard on --hazard-bar (gate node, MUTATION OFF chip, blocked execute).
- Liveness (all guarded): the .wf-anim class is only added when !reduceMotion; the active node's dot breathes, the spine segment into it flows (animated dashed gradient), the active tool call shows a blinking cursor, the elapsed clock ticks every 1s (both list row and sticky header via data-wf-clock), and every 6s the live run genuinely ADVANCES — the active tool call completes with a duration and the next one from WF_TOOL_POOL is appended (capped at 9 calls). The interval self-clears when the root leaves the DOM, so no route() teardown is required.
- Density: per-node duration bars are sqrt-scaled against the run max for at-a-glance hot-spot reading; dot-leader rules connect labels to the ts/duration column; all evidentiary content (hashes, hosts, queries, results, durations, verdicts) is --mono with tabular-nums.
- Honest preview: list header carries a "FIXTURE DATA" tag; mutation is OFF so completed execute nodes are labeled "execute (dry-run)" with rendered-not-applied payloads, and the POLL_PAUSE run's "approve & execute" button is present but disabled with a
